T l ^ ; g 3 R 0 6 ' T T ^ % 3 ^ ^ ^ 3 r i ^ c l 5 r 3 i k 5 0 f e l I T . %3rftra>35m35T?)M [T&.U 2 7 - 1 5 / 2 0 0 6 - ^ ^ ^ ] -- 3 of 7 -- \'i THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART] r o j femufl ;—IJCT IWT *rrer ^ TT*pre, srarwm ^ IFT I, ^ 2, 3-q-ws (ii) ^ ww *TRCT W&K, ^ ^ (frftfgiM),-3rf^^Tfs^i8-6/70-T^^3nf ^ 6 ^ 2 3 " ^ , 1978 it SRT y+iRw f^ ntj $ sftr ; w t f ^ F ^IFT 3?ft 3TftWTfcTr W T C ?RJ Tfeqi ^T.3?I. 269(3T), cfRfe 26 *?nf, 2001 3T7T wtiTm f ^ *nr ^ M I N I S T R Y O F ENVIRONMENT AND F O R E S T S N O T I F I C A T I O N New Delhi, the 22nd September, 2009 S.O. 2465(E).—Whereas the draft Transport of the Animals Amendment Rules, 2008, were published as required by sub-section (I) of section 38 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, I960 (59 of 1960)under the notification of Government of India, Ministry of Environment and Forest number S.Ol 876(E) dated 31st July 2009 in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3 , Sub Section (ii) dated 3rd August 2009 inviting objections and suggestions from ail persons likely to be affected thereby, before the expiry of the period of thirty days from the date on which copies of the Gazette containing the said notification are made available to the public. And, whereas copies of the said Gazette were made available to the public on the 3"' August 2009. And, whereas no objection or suggestion has been received from the public in respect of the said draft rules by the Central Government. Now, therefore in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-sections (1) and (2) of section 38 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, I960 (59 of 1960), the Central Government hereby makes the following. rules further to amend the Transport of Animals Rules 1978, namely:- 1. (I) These rules 'may be called Transport of Animals (Amendment) Rules, 2009. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Transport of Animals Rules, 1978 (herein after referred to as the said rules), for rule 50, the following rule 50 shall be substituted, namely:- "50. ..The average space provided per cattle in Railway wagon or vehicle shall be.as provided in Tables I and II as given below:- . . T A B L E - I Space Allowance per Cattle Cattle weighing upto 200Kg Cattle weighing 200-300 Kg Cattle weighing 300-400 Kg Cattle Weighing above 400 Kg 1 Square.Meter 1.20 Square.Meter 1.40 Square.Meter 2.0 Square Meter C S s m . # -- 4 of 7 -- TABLE-II Space requirement for Cattle while being transported in commonly sized road vehicles Vehicle Size Length x Width Square Meter G 9 < 2 4 56 X 2 3 4 16X1.9 ? 9X< 89 Floor Area of tne vehicle in Sqjare Meter (Sq mlr) 1656 1288 7.904 5.481 Number of Cattle Cattle weighing upto 200 Kg (1 Sqmtr space per cattle) 16 1? 8 5 Catlle weighing 200-300 Kg (1 20 Sq.mtr space per cattle) 14 10 6 4 Cattle weighing 300-400Kg (1.40 Sq.mtr space per cattle) 12 8 6 4 Cattle weighing abjve 403Kg (2.0 Sq.mtr space per calile) 8 6 4 ? In the said rules, in rule 94, after sub rule (a), in the TABLE, under the heading Broad gauge, in column (1), for the entry, the following entry shall be substituted namely:- Broad gauge (1) "Area of Wagon_ "VPU"~HavTng" Floor Area 63.5Square Meter Number of Pigs 104 (0.61 Square Meter per Pig) 6. In the said rules, in rule 95, for the clause (a), the following clause shall be substituted, namely:- "(a) Maximum Number of Pigs permitted for commonly sized road vehicle b.iNo. i i I 1. ? ] Type of Animal Weaner Young Adult Maximum Vehicle Number of P igs Permitted for road vehicles Vehicle having size j having size 5.6m x 2.35m j 5.15m x 43 31 " " 2)" 2.! 8m 1- 3 7 26 " 18 Vehicle having size Vehicle having size 3.03m x ; 2.9m x 2.18m | 2.0m ^ "22 ! 19" r 15' 1 " 13 10 " 9 " -- 6 of 7 -- [MTTII—TI^3(ii)] TO ^T Wm : smWUH 7 Not_e_£— For the purpose of Pigs of all breeds, ages and sex. the following Space allowances shall apply: (d) Weaner- Piglet which has just been separated from the mother for the purpose of independent rearing and commonly in the weight range of 12Kg - 15Kg. (e) Young -- Maie or female pig between 03 to 06 months of age and commonly in the weight range of 15Kg ~ 50Kg. (i) Adult - A Male or female pig above 06 months of age and having weight more than 50Kg.". [F,No.27-15/2006-AWD] HEM PANDE, Jt. Secy. (AW) ]y0te : The Principal rules were published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, vide number Part 1, Section 2 (ii) vide Government of India, Ministry of Agriculture & Irrigation (Department of Agriculture), Notification No. 18-6/70-LDI dated 23rd March 1978 and subsequently amended by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment vide number S.O.269 (E) dated 26th March, 2001.
